full_prompt: Design the primary structure cylinder for an national CanSat-class benchmark-26 entry. Guidelines pin mass at 1.000 kg +/- 0.050 kg, envelope a cylinder of 150 mm diameter x 400 mm length, launched to 1000 m apogee by a model rocket, descending under parachute at ~5 m/s. Onboard sensor suite comprises pressure/temperature/IMU; battery, radio, and OBC take the remaining volume. The primary structure is a single-piece machined cylinder that houses three internal bulkheads to support sensor PCBs and parachute bay. Geometry: OD 150.0 mm (+0/-0.2 mm fairing fit), ID 146.0 mm (wall 2 mm nominal), total length 400 mm, with three internal bulkhead rings at 100 mm, 200 mm, and 300 mm from the base; each bulkhead is 3 mm thick, features four M3 threaded bosses for PCB standoffs on an 80 mm square pattern, and a central 40 mm diameter cable pass-through hole. Upper end cap is a separate 8 mm thick aluminium disc with 12 M3 through-holes on a 135 mm bolt circle for parachute bridle attachment (3 bridle tabs) and 3 igniter access ports. Lower end cap is a 5 mm thick aluminium disc with 4 M3 mounting holes for the ejection pusher rod. Material: 6061-T6 aluminium (69 GPa, 0.33, 2700 kg/m^3, 276 MPa yield, 310 MPa UTS). Load cases: LC1 (Launch axial) — 20 g axial compression on full 1 kg payload = 196 N compressive load applied distributed along the cylinder from base to each bulkhead; LC2 (Launch lateral) — 5 g lateral = 49 N distributed; LC3 (Parachute opening shock) — 30 g vertical deceleration on the 1 kg system during chute deploy; apply 294 N upward at the top end cap bolt circle, with base fully fixed; LC4 (Landing impact) — 6 m/s vertical touchdown on grass (modeled as a rigid floor with a 0.05 s stop = ~12 g); apply 117 N axial at the base bulkhead; LC5 (Internal pressure) — a sealed cavity pressurized by ambient descent pressure difference of 0.015 MPa from sea level to 1000 m max; applied as internal pressure on the ID of the cylinder; LC6 (Thermal) — ground launch pad at 45 C surface heating to 60 C on one side, 25 C ambient on the shaded side, steady; linear thermal-structural. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises under LC1, LC2, LC3, LC4 < 184 MPa (yield/1.5); (R2) under LC5, max von Mises < 92 MPa (yield/3, pressure vessel margin); (R3) first-mode linear buckling under LC1 >= 3.0 (thin cylinder in axial compression); (R4) under LC3, max deflection of upper end cap < 3 mm (bridle anchor stability); (R5) under LC6 thermal, max combined stress < 92 MPa; (R6) total primary structure mass (cylinder + 3 bulkheads + 2 end caps) < 300 g so electronics/payload have 700 g budget; (R7) outer diameter under any LC must stay within 150.0 +0/-0.2 mm so the CanSat fits the standard fairing. Primary verification: linear static for LC1, LC2, LC3, LC4, LC5; linear buckling for LC1; thermal-structural for LC6. Vibration modal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Output one STEP AP242 assembly (cylinder + 3 bulkheads + 2 end caps) plus a report listing total primary-structure mass, max stress per case, buckling factor under LC1, end-cap deflection under LC3, and OD change under any LC.
short_prompt:
Design the primary structure cylinder of an India CanSat-class benchmark-26 entry. Wet mass 1.000 kg +/- 0.050 kg; cylindrical 150 mm OD x 400 mm envelope; launched to 1000 m apogee, descending under parachute at ~5 m/s.

Envelope: OD 150.0 mm (+0/-0.2 mm), ID 146.0 mm (2 mm wall), three 3 mm internal bulkheads at 100, 200, 300 mm from the base with 4 x M3 PCB-standoff bosses on an 80 mm square pattern and 40 mm central cable hole; 8 mm top end cap with 12 x M3 on a 135 mm bolt circle (parachute bridle + igniter ports); 5 mm bottom end cap with 4 x M3 for the ejection pusher rod; 6061-T6 throughout.

Factor regime: yield FoS 1.5 for launch/shock/landing; pressure-vessel FoS 3 for the sealed-cavity descent case; linear buckling eigenvalue >= 3.0 under axial launch; OD must stay within 150.0 +0/-0.2 mm.

Load cases.
- Launch axial 20 g on 1 kg distributed along the cylinder.
- Launch lateral 5 g (49 N distributed).
- Parachute-opening shock: 30 g on 1 kg = 294 N upward at the top end-cap bolt circle, base fixed.
- Landing impact 6 m/s on grass = ~12 g = 117 N axial at the base bulkhead.
- Internal pressure: 0.015 MPa differential (sea level to 1000 m).
- Thermal: pad at 45 C with 60 C sunlit side / 25 C shaded side (linear thermo-structural).

prompt.title: national CanSat-class benchmark primary structure
prompt.brief: 1 kg, 150 x 400 mm CanSat primary cylinder with 3 internal bulkheads and 2 end caps, surviving 20 g launch, 30 g chute-deploy shock, and thermal one-sided solar soak.

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Short flight, no sustained vibe qualification required.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Descent aero captured by parachute spec, not body CFD.
